visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video opens with a busy background of plush toys in a warehouse. A large Costco Wholesale logo is centered at the top. Below it, stylized yellow text with a black outline reads "好市多" (Costco). An animated character head resembling Wreck-It Ralph appears on the left with a surprised expression. Text at the bottom indicates a weekly discount report for February 3rd to February 9th. (2) The scene shifts to a series of rapid product showcases. First, Sanrio 18-inch plush toys are shown in a pile; text overlays the screen showing a price drop from 789 to 599. The camera is static, focusing on the products on shelves or in bins within a brightly lit warehouse setting. (3) The sequence continues with Kleenex tissue boxes stacked high on pallets. Yellow text identifies the item as three-layer tissues with a discount from 419 to 335. The background shows typical industrial shelving and concrete floors of a wholesale store. (4) Multiple food items follow in quick succession: Nestle Koko Krunch cereal boxes, Glico Bisco cookies in red boxes, and stacks of Pringles cans. Each product is centered in the frame with clear text overlays detailing the brand name, discount amount, and final promotional price. (5) Household and tech items are introduced, including Philips Sonicare toothbrushes and Soodatek charging cables. The lighting is consistently bright and even, characteristic of a large retail environment. (6) Fresh produce and frozen foods are shown: mesh bags of sweet potatoes, boxes of pears, and cartons of Binggrae milk. The video concludes with high-end health supplements like DHA fish oil and chicken essence, maintaining the same fast-paced editing and text-heavy presentation style.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: No real people are present; only products and graphics.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The background consists of various warehouse retail scenes inside Costco, featuring heavy industrial shelving, pallet-stacked goods, refrigerated displays, and a concrete floor. The environment is orderly and typical of a wholesale club.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) The audio begins with a high-energy, fast-paced male voice introducing the Costco weekly discount report. He speaks with a friendly yet urgent promotional tone. The background music is an upbeat, major-key electronic pop track with a steady 4/4 drum machine beat. (2) As each product appears, the narrator rapidly lists the product name, its original price, the discount amount, and the final promotional price. His articulation is extremely clear, suited for a retail announcement. (3) Subtle 'pop' or 'ding' sound effects are audible at every visual cut, synchronizing with the new text information appearing on screen. The BGM remains consistent in tempo and volume, providing a energetic bed for the narration. (4) The vocal delivery remains high-arousal throughout the entire 87 seconds, with no significant pauses except for very brief breaths between product listings. The audio is studio-quality with high signal-to-noise ratio, focusing entirely on the information delivery.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Itemized Discounts -> Ongoing Promotion
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video serves as a rapid-fire informational guide for Costco Taiwan members, detailing specific product discounts for the week of February 3rd to 9th. It aims to drive foot traffic to the store by highlighting significant price reductions across various categories including food, household items, and electronics.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The entire video is dedicated to showcasing Costco products and their weekly promotional prices, featuring the Costco logo prominently.

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video uses a 'vertical social media' style typical of TikTok or Reels, using high information density and fast cuts to maintain viewer engagement through a long list of items.
